column9: Transfer rate helps estimate remaining time during large pg_basebackup operations

column3: How does PostgreSQL parallelize sequential scans across partitions?
column4: Workers scan different partitions or chunks in parallel
column5: Only the leader scans each partition
column6: Parallel sequential scan is disabled on partitioned tables
column7: Only parallel index scans are allowed
column9: Partitioned tables benefit significantly from parallel sequential scan

column3: What happens when vacuum_cost_page_miss is set very high?
column4: VACUUM pauses less on cache misses → faster vacuum but more aggressive I/O usage
column9: High miss cost removes I/O throttling on cache misses — can starve user queries

column3: What immediate action should be taken if pg_stat_database.datchecksum_failures > 0?
column4: Investigate possible disk corruption or hardware failure immediately
column9: Non-zero checksum failures indicate silent data corruption — urgent action needed

column3: Which pg_stat_progress_vacuum column indicates remaining cleanup effort?
column4: dead_tuples (decreasing value = progress)
column9: Remaining dead tuples gives a sense of how much vacuuming work is left
